questions.text: What PactoSigna document type is used to plan the annual internal audit program?

options.explanation: Per Section 5.1, the audit program is documented as an audit_schedule document type in PactoSigna with structured frontmatter.
Individual audit plans are part of the audit execution, not the annual program. The program uses the audit_schedule document type.
Quality Reviews are for management review, not audit planning.
SOPs define procedures; the audit schedule is a separate document type.

questions.text: What is the key requirement for selecting an internal auditor?

options.text: Must be from the Quality department
options.explanation: Auditors can be from any department as long as they are independent of the area being audited.

options.text: Must be independent of the area being audited
options.explanation: Per Section 5.2, auditors must be independent of the area being audited to ensure objectivity.
Seniority is not a requirement; independence and training are.
Internal audit training on ISO 19011 principles is required, not external certification.

questions.text: Observations identified during an internal audit require a CAPA to be initiated.
questions.explanation: Per Section 5.4, only major and minor findings require CAPA. Observations are opportunities for improvement documented as recommendations, not nonconformances.

questions.text: How does PactoSigna support the connection between audit findings and corrective actions?

options.text: Findings are tracked in a spreadsheet outside the platform
options.explanation: PactoSigna provides integrated tracking within the Quality Events module.

options.text: The Quality Events module links audit findings to CAPA records with full traceability
options.explanation: Per Section 5.6, PactoSigna's Quality Events module links audit findings to CAPA records, maintaining full traceability from finding through corrective action to effectiveness verification.

options.text: Findings are emailed to the CAPA owner for manual entry
options.explanation: The linkage is managed within PactoSigna's integrated Quality Events module.

options.text: There is no connection between audit findings and CAPA
options.explanation: Major and minor findings require CAPA, and PactoSigna links them through the Quality Events module.

questions.text: Within how many business days must the audit report be prepared after audit completion?

options.explanation: 5 days is shorter than the required timeframe.
Per Section 5.5, the Lead Auditor shall prepare the audit report within 10 business days of audit completion.
20 days exceeds the required timeframe.
30 days exceeds the required timeframe.

questions.text: Which PactoSigna features provide evidence for compliance audits?

options.explanation: Multiple features support audit evidence, not just the document list.

options.text: Records Page compiled views, audit trail exports, compliance packages, and training compliance data
options.explanation: Per Section 5.7, PactoSigna supports audits with compliance packages, Records Page views (RMF, V&V, DMR, DHR), audit trail exports, training data, and document status tracking.
The audit trail is one of several evidence sources available in PactoSigna.

options.text: PactoSigna does not provide audit evidence — it must be compiled manually
options.explanation: PactoSigna's compliance reporting module specifically supports audit export and evidence compilation.
